title: Errors 2
description: Functions that can fail should return Result. The caller decides how to handle the error.
instructions: Change the function to return a Result instead of calling unwrap() directly.
hints: `.unwrap()` will panic on an error. Use `?` to propagate it instead.
The `?` operator returns the error to the caller if the Result is Err.
explanation: Using `.unwrap()` panics on error, crashing the program. The `?` operator is the idiomatic way to handle errors - it returns the inner value on `Ok`, or returns the error to the caller on `Err`. The function must return a `Result` to use `?`. This creates a clean chain of error propagation.
